run.head: 59f5d9f80ac3996bb7a29055d91afc44ae49da32
gaps: The read-only filesystem prevented Cargo tests, end-to-end browser tests, and force-red mutation checks from running.
radius impact could not create the repository .radius directory, so no Impact Map or dogfood artifact was produced.
Network access was unavailable for reopening the external Playwright, WAI-ARIA, HTML-ARIA, and Chromium sources named by the getByRole checklist.

lenses.name: Inverse regression surface
lenses.reason: Role lookup changes from DOM selectors to the accessibility tree with a DOM fallback.

lenses.name: New-domain matrix
lenses.reason: Role lookup now accepts implicit roles and additional computed accessibility-tree forms.

lenses.name: Resolution-rule consistency across consumers
lenses.reason: The new DOM fallback resolves ordered role-token lists.

lenses.name: Emission channel and one-shot latch reachability
lenses.reason: No warning channel or one-shot latch is added.

lenses.name: Deliberate-default check
lenses.reason: Default accessible-name matching changes from case-sensitive matching to case-insensitive substring matching.

lenses.name: Fresh-seam scan
lenses.reason: The diff does not patch a runtime or framework to expose a new signal.

lenses.name: Reference-implementation oracle
lenses.reason: The diff reimplements Playwright getByRole and WAI-ARIA semantics.

lenses.name: New-failure-outcome propagation
lenses.reason: The changed handler already returned errors; no formerly success-only or hanging call becomes returnably fallible.

lenses.name: Error-path forcing
lenses.reason: The diff adds accessibility-tree misses, virtual-node errors, DOM fallback misses, and frame-evaluation branches.

lenses.name: Non-destructive recovery
lenses.reason: No heuristic recovery action is added.

lenses.name: Cancellation and timeout hygiene
lenses.reason: No timeout, cancellation mechanism, or independently pending operation is added.

lenses.name: Boundary pipeline trace
lenses.reason: Presentational-role discovery and action execution are extended into selected same-origin and out-of-process frames.

lenses.name: Substrate verification
lenses.reason: The new end-to-end tests claim DOM interaction results and accessibility-tree behavior.

lenses.name: Docs-behavior parity
lenses.reason: The diff changes role matching and edits CLI, MCP, README, docs, skill, and Eve capability descriptions.

lenses.name: Demonstrative example
lenses.reason: The diff adds examples intended to demonstrate implicit-role support.

lenses.name: Complexity budget
lenses.reason: The diff scans every AX node and deduplicates names inside the per-node loop.

deterministic.reason: No added em dash or prohibited comment punctuation was found.
The actions and MCP changes include every required mapped documentation and skill surface.
The diff does not rename or retire a duplicated contract value.
Current-contract passages were inspected; untouched changelog occurrences are historical release records.
Every current file mentioning implicit-role support is included in the diff.
The only dispatch caller is in the changed actions file and performs no state mutation before the call.
The regression tests describe force-red mutations, but the read-only filesystem prevented performing those mutations and running Cargo.
Cargo failed before compilation because it could not create cli/target temporary files.
radius failed because it could not create .radius in the read-only repository.

findings.claim: The advertised find actions type, focus, and uncheck always fail after locator resolution.
findings.evidence: The Eve schema advertises type, focus, and uncheck and forwards each action unchanged, while execute_subaction has arms only for click, fill, check, hover, and text and routes every other value to "Unknown subaction". CLI help and README advertise the same broader action set.

findings.claim: Presentational-role conflict resolution omits two WAI-ARIA 1.2 global properties.
findings.evidence: GLOBAL_ARIA omits aria-braillelabel and aria-brailleroledescription. Therefore an element such as role="none" aria-braillelabel="x" is treated as genuinely presentational even though the global property requires the none/presentation role to be ignored.

findings.claim: The ordered-fallback role set omits the valid WAI-ARIA role suggestion.
findings.evidence: VALID_ROLES contains the other concrete WAI-ARIA 1.2 roles but not suggestion. For role="suggestion none", tokens.find therefore skips the operative suggestion role and incorrectly lets a query for none match the element.

findings.claim: A page-authored data-agent-browser-located attribute can redirect presentational-role actions to the wrong element.
findings.evidence: The fallback marks the matched element but executes the action through document.querySelector("[data-agent-browser-located='true']"). If an earlier element already carries that public attribute, querySelector resolves the earlier element; cleanup also removes the first marker rather than necessarily the marker added by this command.

findings.claim: Failed name queries have quadratic deduplication complexity.
findings.evidence: The loop preserves display order in names_seen while names_set.insert performs expected constant-time membership checks, making the AX-node scan linear in the number of nodes and accumulated name bytes.

findings.claim: Historical changelog passages mentioning getByRole need current semantic wording.
findings.evidence: CHANGELOG.md and docs/src/app/changelog/page.mdx record behavior of prior releases and do not advertise the current command contract.

exemptions.claim: Historical changelog passages mentioning getByRole need current semantic wording.
exemptions.evidence: The changelog occurrences are immutable release history rather than current usage documentation.

issue_candidates.title: Choose and document first-match versus strict-mode role behavior
issue_candidates.evidence: Both the AX path and DOM fallback return the first matching element and never detect multiple matches, diverging from Playwright strict locator actions.
issue_candidates.why_out_of_scope: The supplied house norm classifies strict-mode versus first-match behavior as a genuine product decision.
